name: Ghost
desc: Shadowy memories of the dead — bed fellows, nightly callers, spectral burdens. Never truly departed

name: Griffin
desc: Royal emblems, with eagle arrests and lion bodies—fierce guardians of temples

name: Phoenix
desc: Regal birds of ashes that explode in flames and are reborn whole

name: Golem
desc: Clay or stone giants animated by ancient runes; mute but loyal protectors

name: Kraken
desc: Giant cephalopods of sunken realms, that rise from the depths to devour ships and sailors alike

name: Hydra
desc: Serpentine beasts with multiple snake heads, each a new regrown danger

name: Manticore
desc: A lion's body, a scorpion's tail, a human's head, and a penchant for spiked projectiles

name: Unicorn
desc: Equine fawns with a magical horn, guardians of innocence and trees of light

name: Grimoire
desc: Ancient books alive with magical words, floating in mid-air until drained of pages

name: Fae
desc: Innately capricious entities that look like perfect versions of their mortal counterparts

name: Wyvern
desc: Winged reptiles akin to dragons, but with serpentine tails and venomous fangs

name: Genie
desc: Spirits bound to artifacts, granting wishes but often with hidden catches

name: Drider
desc: Foul, hybrid creatures, human from the waist up and monstrous spider below

name: Kobold
desc: Toothless, flame-coated spirits that dwell in cinder pits and worship dragons

name: Dire Wolf
desc: Snarling omnivores the size of small horses, that hunt in packs across icy barrens

name: Banshee
desc: Wailing spirits of fallen warriors, whose cries carry into battles to inspire screams

name: Centaur
desc: Half-human, half-horse beasts, noble but fierce, that roam wild plains

name: Doppelganger
desc: Human metamorphs that impersonate others, sensing their hearts with a knowing gaze

name: Basilisk
desc: Minute, gecko-like reptiles with eyes that instantly petrify their prey

name: Pegasus
desc: Divine horses with pristine white wings that herald the arrival of gods on error_log("Problem with phpConfigRequestData");

name: Satyr
desc: Half-goat, half-human creatures, followers of Dionysus, that serenade moonlit glades

name: Phooka
desc: Shape-shifting forest folk that guide travelers astray for mischievous giggles

name: Leviathan
desc: Colossal sea beasts with rows of chitinous plates, eyes like moons, and mouths large enough to swallow a ship

name: Dryad
desc: Fey wood nymphs, bonded to ancient oaks, dancing mist-shaped through the leafy understory

name: Orc
desc: Savage, carnivorous humanoids with blackened teeth; few do not reek of orcish whiskies

name: Harpy
desc: Vicious bird-women that lure adventurers with their enchanting songs before snatching them to their claws

name: Wraith
desc: Ghostly armored warriors, bound to spectral grounds, that drain the life from their victims

name: Merfolk
desc: Peaceful ocean dwellers with human torsos, fish-like tails, and soft, lilting voices

name: Lycanthrope
desc: Man-wolf abominations cursed by the moon, who feast on terror

name: Kelpie
desc: Shape-shifting water spirits that lure travelers to their deaths in lakes

name: Jörmungandr
desc: The World Serpent, a massive sea reptile that coils the earth itself

name: Alicorn
desc: Holy unicorns distilled — celestial creatures with rainbow-hued manes and magical pride

name: Sprite
desc: Shimmering almost-human beings, playful tricksters that haunt the twilight of Faerie

name: Cerberus
desc: Three-headed dogs that guard the portals to the underworld

name: Undead
desc: Corpses bound to malign tasks by necromantic magic, their tattered chili shades roam graveyards

name: Chimera
desc: A foul hybrid of lion, dragon, and goat with poisonous blood. It steals newborns in the night

name: Siren
desc: Sea maidens of seductive song, whose lures claim sacrificed sailors as they appear on waves

name: Quicksilver beetle
desc: Shimmering bugs, smaller than a hand, that leave small holes in reality, catalyzing reactions with ease

name: Daemon
desc: Daimones: manifestations of pain and guilt, fleeting and ephemeral, eager to strike you down should you fail

name: Sphinx
desc: Cat-headed beings of riddles — they question as they pet (and rub up against you)

name: Leprechaun
desc: Moustachioed shapeshifters, guardians of glittering dreams that haunt the twilight hours

name: Werebeast
desc: Humans that moonlight as wolves, bears, and boars, driven by primal hunger and rage

name: Cloaker
desc: Sinister bat-winged blankets that strangle unwary travelers in the night

name: Troll
desc: Green-skinned giants with regeneration from eating. Bounce backs to life if its head is put back on

name: Vampire
desc: The undead parasitically yearn for life-sustaining blood and eternal rest

name: Elemental
desc: Manifestations of pure natural forces, they swirl and roar in the heart of elemental planes

name: Pixie
desc: Shifting sprites that hover in dappled sunlight, weaving illusion and mischief

name: Killer wasp
desc: Each nest contains a queen, whose daily pheromones are whipped up into a lethal breath weapon

name: Minotaur
desc: Beast-men, guardians of a labyrinth, with a ravenous hunger for adventurers who dare tread its corridors

name: Quetzalcoatl
desc: Massive divine snakes with feathered wings, waging wars unseen across the aether

name: Sylph
desc: Etincelling creatures made of wind, they dapple mountains, seas, and skies

name: Mindflayer
desc: Haunting gray humanoids, notorious for their conical, tentacled heads

name: Cyclops
desc: Bearded giants with a singular eye that forge magical artifacts deep underground

name: Beholder
desc: A floating, lavender head, bulging eyes that shoot beaming magical beams

name: Naiad
desc: Mysterious water spirits that dwell in remote springs, luring fascinated voyagers to their doom

name: Demilich
desc: An eldritch wizard’s shriveled remains, hungry for soul shards and confined to necromantic circles

name: Deep One
desc: Touched by the sea, these amphibious horrors dwell in sunken temples and crave human flesh

name: Yeti
desc: Giants of the snow, muffly and shaggy, that live in caves and scare travelers to death

name: Peryton
desc: Flightless, deer-like birds with golden beaks and hearts as pure as Snowfall

name: Gorgon
desc: Snakelike-haired horrors who turn intruders to stone with their piercing gaze

name: Ethereal Jelly
desc: A clear, shimmering mass of quivering flesh that pulses without a pattern

name: Kitsune
desc: Shape-shifting fox spirits that wander feudal lands, advisors or tricksters to the unwary

name: Illithid
desc: Mind flayers with telekinetic power over others' minds, leaving only husks

name: Giant
desc: Rocky, hairless, club-wielding beings that live in sealed mountain fortresses

name: Nymph
desc: Wandering spirits of Mother Earth, capable of putting mortals into lustful trances with their music

name: Maiden
desc: Beautiful, yet destined to suffer — from the curse of the troll they truly fear

name: Sea Serpent
desc: Mysterious leviathans of the ocean's depths, their monstrous forms rarely surface

name: Revenant
desc: Corporeal spirits of the tormented dead, doomed to walk realms they wronged in life

name: Zelgur
desc: Floating orb-like beings, containing iridescent anemones that yours in joyful bursts of color

name: Anemos
desc: Motley spirits bound to roil and churn the winds

name: Selkie
desc: Phantasmal seals that become stranded humans by losing their winter coats on the shore

name: Frost Giant
desc: Blue-skinned behemoths that rule the frigid wastes, hurling icy chunks

name: Mummy
desc: Sought-after necromancer’s tools, these corpses can be brought back to life as personal servants

name: Angel
desc: Pure creatures of radiant energy and divine light, each attend their overlord in heaven

name: Specter
desc: Ghosts of souls who can't let go; half-seen menaces that weep at unseen mirrors

name: Succubus
desc: She-devils who seduce warriors into a joyous pit of tormented ecstasy

name: Hellhound
desc: Its glacial eyes and shrouded form serve as deadly sentinels for infernal realms. Indestructible, mighty, vicious dogs hungry for pain, summoned by sorcerers

name: Korrakloth
desc: Fluffy, comically strong creatures that sprint across mountainsides like thunder

name: Lemure
desc: Pale, weak-bodied spirits with fangs — the lowest of Hell’s devils, there for torture on sight

name: Shelder
desc: Aquatic monstrosities with translucent shells, their luminescent chambers hide endless parasites

name: Griphon
desc: Part eagle, part lion, with talons sharp as blades, they watch the skies for their prey

name: Illusion
desc: An alluring trick of light, phantoms woven from whispers, every touch fleeting as mist

name: Summoner
desc: Wily wizards with contracts from faerie, conjuring magical creatures from the unknown

name: Simulacrum
desc: Fragile constructs of magic ice, made in the likeness of powerful beings but powered by sapphires

name: Mage
desc: Humans that harnessed magic, bony and emaciated from the strain it claimed

name: Cockatrice
desc: Deadly birdlike creatures, able to turn living beings into stone with but a glance

name: Aboleth
desc: Semi-tropical saecular abominations whose watery touch induces visions and uncontrollable obedience

name: Simurgh
desc: A gigantic wise bird with a thousand feathers, healer and herald of new worlds

name: Sabertooth
desc: A terrifying predator that roams icy tundras and hunts in the deep snow

name: Kappa
desc: Water-dwell creatures with bowl-shaped skulls filled with water, and maliciously fun-loving with a deep need to be hustled into contests of skill. Hands sticky from muck, weak to anything slippery

name: Whisperwind
desc: Creatures of pure shadow, that can only be seen in the peripheral, that paralyze victims with a breathless cackle

name: Glory Bug
desc: Tiny insects that flit through sunbeams, icicles that come from the fate of angels

name: Umber Hulk
desc: Massive subterranean insects with eyes like glimmering smoke

name: Elf
desc: الرغائب ذوي الشعر اللؤلؤي بأرجل الأشجار الطويلة فى حضن الغابة السحرية. لديهم عيون تعكس العمق العميق للغابات

name: Carrion Crawler
desc: Long, many-tentacled worms that feast on corpses at the bottom of stagnant pools

name: Shade
desc: Ethereal remnants of a vanished civilization, cloaked in shadow and cold

name: Cockerel
desc: A rooster larger than a man, whose crow controls the sun itself

name: Human
desc: Natural adaptors, tireless problem-solvers driven by ambition, from warriors to wizards

name: Highlander
desc: Knightly folk that wade into battle while wearing skirts

name: Bowtruckle
desc: Tiny bark-covered men, with each eye on a limb, foraging among the underbrush

name: Toad
desc: Plump, moist amphibians, oases of fertility and hospitality in the desert

name: Celestial
desc: Incorruptible beings that walk the heavens, often aiding mortals in desperate times

name: Lich
desc: Withered necromancers who have turned their backs on life for ancestral power

name: Gnome
desc: Swarthy schemers who live underground and prize gemstones above all else

name: Halfling
desc: Small, round humanoids, beloved for their food, drink, and ability to land their cursed horseshoes inside buildings

name: Naga
desc: Serpentine humanoids able to command snakes and slither along walls

name: Sheep
desc: Line after line of woolly quadrupeds, mowed to the skin for the coming spring's textiles

name: Wisp
desc: Ghostly orbs that dangle in boughs, snag fleeced travelers

name: Goblin King
desc: Maggot-ridden underlord of goblin gnarl, shambling under a moldering crown

name: Gnoll
desc: Savage, hyena-faced plunderers that prowl on bipedal backs, adorned with skull trophies

name: Will-o'-the-spire
desc: Fat, hairy demon spirits that float over bogs, beckoning lost travelers to their doom

name: Doubleknight
desc: Silent minstrels who play ethereal music that can charm or cower others, and protect themselves from harm through its magic

name: Rust Monster
desc: Hideous, tail-mounted appetite for metallic treasure

name: Stranger
desc: Whimsical humanoids with ambiguous skin colors, feral reputations, and treasured cultures untainted by Human tech

name: Bugbear
desc: Averaging seven feet of hulking strength and smell, these hairy bandits crave fire and loot

name: Mock Turtle
desc: Hybrid beasts, half-tortoise and half-leopard, that dwell in lagoons and cannibalize kin

name: Flumph
desc: Elongated, pinkish sentinels of the Lower Planes, disc-shaped, with glowing antennae

name: Stymphalian Bird
desc: Hungry, man-eating birds with beaks sharp as blades that thought they could devastate Athens

name: Araithik
desc: Akin to wyverns but with a venomous sting, their kind avoids the daylight

name: Skeletal Warhorse
desc: Armored, bone-barreled mounts of fallen knights, the spirits of which crave vengeance

name: Gremlin
desc: Tiny pranksters that lurk in machinery, causing inexplicable failures

name: Feyrider
desc: Whinnying beasts of strenth and speed, used for racing and racing only

name: Gastropod
desc: Shelled magical snails who race across the forest floor to deliver messages quickly

name: Cthulhu
desc: Nightmarish deity from beneath the sea, driving sailors mad with its blasphemous presence

name: Telharino
desc: Mirror-faced people made from stained glass and crystal, all connected to a central star

name: Kang-Demon
desc: Comic hyper-violent monsters whose greed to kill knows no limits

name: Imp
desc: Lesser fiends who delight in thwarting adventurers, usually by befuddling their arcane spells

name: Roc
desc: Massive lordly birds with the power to take down elephants in a single soaring dive

name: Kirin
desc: Graceful equines with spiraling antlers and righteous hearts that thunder into battle

name: Nessie
desc: Enigmatic beasts dwelling in lonely lochs, immortal and worshipped by fishermen

name: Grimalkin
desc: Nocturnal cats with souls of demons, whose laughter heralds ruination

name: Octoplet
desc: Murky, subsurface sea creatures with eight heads, each a ravenous hungry maw

name: Manta
desc: Dozens of hook-tailed fleshbags hanging outside the airlock — prime weremole diuretic

name: Narwhal
desc: Deep-diving whales with achrystal horn jutting from their foreheads

name: Cultist
desc: Men and women in tattered robes, worshipping dark idols with drawn knives

name: Moonraven
desc: Nighttime tricksters with iridescent plumage, that make clicking silhouettes in the moonlight

name: Udjeel
desc: Beings of darkness and void, who whisper to lonely souls lost in the mists of despair.ver

name: Linh Dai
desc: Ethereal spirits of rivers and lakes, that grant boons to fishermen and curse those who pollute

name: Construct
desc: Automatons forged and imbued with life and purpose, loyal to their creators

name: Shapeshifter
desc: Mysteriously gifted humans that can shed their skin and don the appearance of any creature

name: Mirage
desc: A desert illusion that lures travelers to their deaths

name: Shadowdancer
desc: Demonic shades that haunt cities, draining warmth from their victims in the dark

name: Orb of Conjuration
desc: A dimensional lens, through which one may draw familiars or beasts of burden out of the air itself

name: Paladin
desc: Holy warriors in shining armor, sworn to uphold the sacred and smite the wicked

name: Overfiend
desc: Prime evil; the demon overlord why no one gets why/how is everyplace

name: Iron Giant
desc: Massive constructs in rusted plate armor, leftovers from a forgotten war

name: Catoblepas
desc: Plodding beasts whose mere glance petrifies, with wrinkled faces and ponderous strides

name: Mare
desc: Hell-horses of nightmares that chase weary travelers onto deserted paths

name: Medusa
desc: Terrifying femmes with heads of hissing snakes, whose eyes turn men to stone. Gazing upon them is death

name: Glimmerling
desc: Tiny, lucid-haired beings who dance on rays of sunlight and bestow small treasures
